Order line item: The table stores individual line items for a sales order, linking to the order via ord_id and to the product via prod_id, with quantity (qty) and monetary amounts (amt, disc_amt). It also includes line-level fulfillment status (line_status) and denormalized product fields captured at order time (product_code, item_name), which are typical attributes of an order line item.

Product Catalogue: The table defines core product master data including SKU, product name, description, unit price, stock quantity, category, brand, and lifecycle dates (introduced/discontinued) with an active flag. Product inventory movement: The table records product quantity changes (qty) over time (txn_dt) and distinguishes transaction directions/types via txn_type, which includes receipts (IN), shipments/other outflows (OUT), and corrections (ADJ). Because receipts are explicitly included, the table implements a broader concept of inventory movements rather than adjustments only.

Order Status History: The table stores OldStatus/NewStatus along with a timestamp (ChangedDate) and user (ChangedByUser) tied to OrderID, which directly represents historical changes in an order’s workflow status. The table purpose and naming explicitly indicate it is an audit log of order status changes.
